articles.summary: Israeli defence minister seeks pardon for soldier who killed Palestinian Israeli  Defence Minister Israel Katz has requested that President Isaac Herzog pardon Elor Azaria, a former Israeli soldier convicted for killing an incapacitated  Palestinian  in Hebron, Israeli media reported. On 24 March 2016, Azaria killed Abdel Fattah al-Sharif while he was lying wounded and motionless on the ground, 11 minutes after he was shot by Israeli troops due to an alleged stabbing attempt.  Around one year later, Azaria was convicted of manslaughter, for which he was sentenced to 18 mont

articles.title: European regulator advises airlines to avoid Bahrain, Qatar and UAE airspace
articles.summary: European regulator advises airlines to avoid Bahrain, Qatar and UAE airspace The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) on Tuesday issued an advisory for airlines not to operate over Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates and areas near the Gulf of Oman. Citing heightened security risks linked to regional military activity, EASA, in a statement, said that "potential use of missiles, drones, combat aircraft and air-defence systems poses a high risk to civilian flights." Last week, the agency had issued similar guidelines for the airspace of Iran, Iraq and Lebanon.

articles.title: Four sites in Bushehr targeted by US strikes, Iranian official says
articles.summary: Four sites in Bushehr targeted by US strikes, Iranian official says US air strikes hit four locations in the southern Iranian city of Bushehr, Iran’s official news agency IRNA reported, quoting the province’s deputy governor. Iranian officials also said that US strikes targeted the cities of Abadan and Mahshahr in Khuzestan province.  The attacks struck the two cities on Monday afternoon, the report quoted Khuzestan Deputy Governor Valiollah Hayati as saying.  Abadan is home to the Middle East’s oldest oil refinery, while Mahshahr is a key port city on Iran’s Gulf coast.

articles.title: Israel to hold national elections on 27 October, Knesset says
articles.summary: Israel to hold national elections on 27 October, Knesset says Israel’s parliament will dissolve on 17 July and national elections will be held on 27 October as scheduled, according to the Knesset's legal adviser Sagit Afik. Times of Israel reported that the vote would mark Israel’s first election held on schedule since 1988 and make Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s government the first Israeli administration to complete a full term since 1973. The current coalition was formed in December 2022 following the collapse of the Naftali Bennett's Yair Lapid government.
